questions.question: What is self-consistency's structural limit as the single-model baseline?
options: All N samples come from one base model, so errors are correlated by construction — a systematic bias is shared by every sample and survives the vote
questions.explanation: Wang et al. 2022 got big GSM8K gains with N=40 samples, but the i.i.d.-from-one-model design means majority voting can converge confidently on the model's own bias. Heterogeneous multi-agent voting exists to break that correlation.

questions.question: Which four structural choices determine whether debate helps or hurts?
options: Model size, GPU count, batch size, learning rate
Topology (who talks to whom), round count, heterogeneity (different base models), and whether an adversarial voice is present
questions.explanation: Teams that bolt "run 5 agents and vote" onto a task often regress versus a single agent — and the failures track these axes, not randomness. The MAD survey confirms: debate variants structurally similar to self-consistency underperform it at equal budget.

questions.question: Per MultiAgentBench, which topology wins on which task type?
options: Graph wins research (any-to-any critique), star wins fast factual answers (hub consolidates), chain wins stepwise pipelines
questions.explanation: The ACL 2025 benchmark (MARBLE) measured star/chain/tree/graph across research, coding, and planning. Match the information-flow shape to the task shape — there is no universal winner.

questions.question: What is the "coordination tax," and why does it bite past ~4 agents in graph topology?
options: Wall-clock and token cost grow faster than quality — each agent's context fills with peers' outputs, so the marginal value of agent N+1 drops once everyone sees everyone
A regulatory limit on agent count
questions.explanation: The 4-agent ceiling is empirical, not fundamental — it reflects 2026 context capacity. Fully-connected debate pays quadratic-ish context costs for diminishing critique value.

questions.question: What two emergent behaviors did AgentVerse document, and why is one double-edged?
options: Volunteer behaviors and conformity behaviors — conformity helps find consensus but is also the seed of groupthink
questions.explanation: Conformity is a feature when it drives convergence and a risk when it suppresses dissent — the groupthink failure mode the later failure-modes lesson covers. Adversarial structure (one agent must argue against) is the standard counterweight.
